quote: Cutting losses quickly is one of the most important rules of stock trading. Even the best traders are wrong a lot of the time.
It's not whether you're right or wrong, but how much money you make when you're right and how much you lose when you're wrong.
Trade what you see, not what you think.
It takes courage to be a pig.
Risk management is the most important thing to be well understood. It's a way to ensure ongoing survival.
The whole secret to winning big in the stock market is not how much you make on your winners, but how much you don't lose on your losers.
I look for stocks making new highs, not new lows.
I am much more interested in 'when' than 'why'. I don't need to know why a stock should go up; all I need to know is whether it is likely to go up.
The big money is made by sitting and being patient, not by trading every day.
